title: First two characters are swapped in 'podman exec' streams, but not 'podman run'
body:
After entering a toolbox the first character isn't displayed and instead is inserted after the second character is input. This happens on BASH and ZSH and with a test user with a vanilla homedir.

Fedora Silverblue 31, toolbox 0.0.17

Example: after typing "exit" immediately following "toolbox enter"
![Screenshot from 2020-01-11 21-44-10](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8332322/72214273-e7296580-34bb-11ea-887b-7cc0936a58fa.png)

comments.body:
Yes, I can reproduce with:
```
$ podman create --name foo registry.fedoraproject.org/f31/fedora-toolbox:31 sleep +Inf
$ podman start foo
$ podman exec --env TERM=$TERM -it foo /bin/bash
...
```

However, the equivalent `run` command works fine:
```
$ podman run -it --rm registry.fedoraproject.org/f31/fedora-toolbox:31 /bin/bash
```

I wonder if this is related to https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4400.

I have `podman-1.7.0-2.fc31.x86_64`. What version do you have?
oh, hooray I already fixed this in https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/4818 :smile: can you try latest master @debarshiray ?
I think a better question now might be what did #4818 not fix - great work @haircommander
We should cut a 1.7.1 after Devconf.cz to get this out into the wild

> oh, hooray I already fixed this in #4818 smile can you try latest master @debarshiray ?

Confirmed fixed in master. I tested the snapshot at commit d52132b6ecc1c1c9fb955ecee629f8d169ef1119
